item.title: Sri Lanka discloses another missing payment, days after hackers stole $2.5M from its finance ministry
item.dc:creator: Zack Whittaker
item.description: The government of Sri Lanka has lost more than $3 million in two recent, separate cybersecurity incidents as the country continues to recover from its 2022 debt crisis.

item.title: Firestorm Labs raises $82M to take drone factories into the field
item.dc:creator: Kate Park
item.description: A defense startup just raised $82 million to put drone factories inside shipping containers and bring manufacturing to the front lines.

item.title: BMW i Ventures has a new $300M fund and AI is riding shotgun
item.dc:creator: Kirsten Korosec
item.description: BMW i Ventures is interested in startups working on agentic AI and physical AI, as well as industrial software, advanced materials, and manufacturing and supply chain technologies.

item.title: Meet Shapes, the app bringing humans and AI into the same group chats
item.dc:creator: Aisha Malik
item.description: Think Discord chats, but with AI characters in addition to humans.

item.title: Google Translate now lets you practice pronunciation
item.dc:creator: Ivan Mehta
item.description: The feature is rolling out in the U.S. and India with support for English, Spanish, and Hindi.

item.title: Jack Dorsey-backed Vine reboot Divine launches to the public
item.dc:creator: Sarah Perez
item.description: Divine, a Vine reboot backed by Jack Dorsey’s nonprofit, revives six-second looping videos.

item.title: Colby Adcock’s Scout AI raises $100M to train its models for war. We visited its bootcamp
item.dc:creator: Tim Fernholz
item.description: We visited Scout AI's training ground where it's working on AI agents that can help individual soldiers control fleets of autonomous vehicles.

item.title: How one venture firm is investing in an increasingly fragmented world
item.dc:creator: Tim De Chant
item.description: Geopolitical turmoil has made venture investing challenging, leading Kompas VC to carve out a niche in startups focused on the physical world.

item.title: At his OpenAI trial, Musk relitigates an old friendship
item.dc:creator: Connie Loizos
item.description: It's a story Musk has told before -- in interviews and to author Walter Isaacson for his bestselling biography of Musk -- but Tuesday was the first time he said it under oath.

item.title: Amazon is already offering new OpenAI products on AWS
item.dc:creator: Julie Bort
item.description: A day after OpenAI got Microsoft to agree to end exclusive rights, AWS announced a slate of OpenAI model offerings, including a new agent service.

item.title: Amazon launches an AI-powered audio Q&A experience on product pages
item.dc:creator: Lauren Forristal
item.description: Amazon's new "Join the chat" feature lets you ask questions about products and receive AI-powered audio responses.

item.title: Match Group invests $100M in Sniffies, a cruising app for gay men
item.dc:creator: Lucas Ropek
item.description: The app is Match Group's newest attempt to get mobile users excited about online romance again.

item.title: Google expands Pentagon’s access to its AI after Anthropic’s refusal
item.dc:creator: Julie Bort
item.description: After Anthropic refused to allow the DoD to use its AI for domestic mass surveillance and autonomous weapons, Google has signed a new contract with the department.

item.title: Paragon is not collaborating with Italian authorities probing spyware attacks, report says
item.dc:creator: Lorenzo Franceschi-Bicchierai
item.description: Despite promising to help determine what happened with the hacks targeting journalists and activists in Italy, Israeli American spyware maker Paragon has reportedly not responded to authorities’ requests for information.

item.title: US Supreme Court appears split over controversial use of ‘geofence’ search warrants
item.dc:creator: Zack Whittaker
item.description: The U.S. top court is expected to rule on whether to allow police to identify criminal suspects by dragnet searching the databases of tech giants.

item.title: Australia forces Big Tech firms to pay for news or face a 2.25% tax
item.dc:creator: Kate Park
item.description: The more deals platforms make with media outlets, the less they pay. If enough agreements go through, that effective rate drops to 1.5%, which could generate between A$200 million and A$250 million back into Australian journalism.

item.title: Lovable launches its vibe-coding app on iOS and Android
item.dc:creator: Sarah Perez
item.description: The app allows developers to vibe code web apps and websites on the go.

item.title: Founder of Shark Tank-backed startup Scholly sues his acquirer Sallie Mae
item.dc:creator: Dominic-Madori Davis, Sean O'Kane
item.description: Chris Gray is suing his startup’s acquirer, Sallie Mae, for wrongful termination and alleging it's selling student data through a subsidiary. Sallie Mae denies the allegations and vows to fight.

item.title: Apple introduces a cheaper option for App Store subscriptions
item.dc:creator: Sarah Perez
item.description: Apple is adding a new subscription option that lets app developers offer lower monthly pricing in exchange for a 12-month commitment.

item.title: Snapchat brings AI-powered conversational advertising to its app
item.dc:creator: Aisha Malik
item.description: Users will be able to chat with a brand's AI agent to do things like ask questions and get recommendations.
